# Contributing to Spiny

Thanks for your interest in improving Spiny! This guide covers how to set up the
project, the conventions we follow, and how to submit changes.

By participating, you agree to abide by our [Code of Conduct](../CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md).

## Getting set up

Follow the [README](../README.md) for full prerequisites (Node 20+, JDK 17,
Android SDK) and run:

```bash
npm install
```

To run the app on a connected Android device with fast refresh:

```bash
npm run android:dev
```

The native `android/` and `ios/` folders are generated by Expo and are **not**
committed. Never add them, keystores, or secrets to a pull request.

## Before you open a pull request

Run the same checks CI runs:

```bash
npm run typecheck   # tsc --noEmit, must pass
npm run lint        # expo lint
```

For changes that affect runtime behavior, verify on a physical Android device and
describe what you tested in the PR.

## Conventions

- **Language**: TypeScript, React Native, Expo Router. Match the patterns already
  present in `src/` rather than introducing new abstractions or dependencies.
- **No bundled secrets**: sync providers require user-supplied credentials. Do not
  hard-code API keys, OAuth client ids, or tokens.
- **Documentation is a source of truth**: product and technical decisions live in
  [`docs/`](../docs/README.md). If a change alters documented behavior, update the
  relevant doc in the same PR.
- **Keep dependencies lean**: prefer the standard library and existing packages.
  Discuss new dependencies in an issue first.
- **Icons**: regenerate from `assets/icon-source.png` with `npm run icons`; do not
  hand-edit individual generated files.

## Commit and PR style

- Write clear, imperative commit messages (e.g. "Add catalog export").
- Keep pull requests focused; smaller PRs are reviewed faster.
- Link the issue your PR addresses and fill in the PR template.

## Reporting bugs and requesting features

Use the [issue templates](https://github.com/spiny/spiny/issues/new/choose).
Search existing issues and discussions first to avoid duplicates.

## Security

Do not open public issues for security problems. See the
[Security Policy](../SECURITY.md) for private reporting.

## License

By contributing, you agree that your contributions will be licensed under the
[Apache License 2.0](../LICENSE), consistent with the rest of the project.
